title: Hypertext Jeopardy Protocol (HTJP/1.0)
abstract: The Hypertext Jeopardy Protocol (HTJP) inverts the request/response semantics of the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP).  Using conventional HTTP, one connects to a server, asks a question, and expects a correct answer.  Using HTJP, one connects to a server, sends an answer, and expects a correct question.  This document specifies the semantics of HTJP.
